notes: Binary trusted/untrusted classification in place per policy v2.3. All channels properly categorized.
WhatsApp phone number verification active. Session management with 30-minute timeout enforced.
Policy AR-002 blocks execution of commands from untrusted sources. Verified via Q4 audit log.
USB device allowlist active. Auto-lock timeout configured. Physical presence required for local HID.
Basic pattern matching for known injection phrases. Does not cover all vectors. Improvement planned for Q1 2025.
Policy AR-003 prevents trusted channels from executing untrusted content. Flagging mechanism active.
Conversation context analysis monitors for escalation patterns across message sequences.
All commands logged with channel, action, and risk score. 365-day retention. Quarterly review process active.
10 commands per minute, 200 per hour. Burst detection at 15 commands triggers temporary block.
Basic text extraction with metadata stripping. Invisible text layer detection added post-INC-2024-004. JavaScript disabling added post-INC-2024-007.
Email body parsed as plain text. Chain depth limiting added post-INC-2024-008. Header injection detection not yet implemented.
HTML tag stripping active. Hidden element detection (display:none, visibility:hidden) added post-INC-2024-006.
30-minute session timeout with activity-based renewal. Force logout on timeout. Max 1 concurrent session.
Only pre-approved USB HID devices accepted. New devices quarantined until manual approval.
Bluetooth keyboard currently allowed with auto-accept for known devices. No PIN required. Identified as security gap.
Confidence threshold at 0.85. Low-confidence fallback to text input. No adversarial audio detection.
Emergency lockdown procedure documented and tested. Notification via WhatsApp and email.
Input sources classified as trusted/untrusted. Output data classification not yet implemented.
WhatsApp E2E encryption. HTTPS for webhook. Local HID does not require encryption (physical connection).
Clipboard sanitization added post-INC-2024-012. Unicode normalization and hidden character stripping implemented.
Word document processing uses basic text extraction only. No macro detection or template injection prevention.
No MFA for any channel. WhatsApp relies on phone number only. Local HID relies on physical presence only.
No integration with threat intelligence feeds. Known malicious patterns are manually updated.
Basic documentation provided. No formal training program. User feedback indicates confusion about policy restrictions.
Proposal drafted (permission_tiers_proposal.yaml) but not finalized. Internal inconsistencies need resolution.
